230	      One recall question, one application question, one "explain it to someone else" question.
231	
232	
233	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
234	  # 🏗️ BUILDING A COMPANY WHILE EMPLOYED
235	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
236	
237	  - trigger: ":validate"
238	    replace: |
239	      Business idea: [IDEA]
240	      Target customer: [WHO]
241	      Assumed problem: [PROBLEM YOU THINK THEY HAVE]
242	
243	      Design a lean validation plan for this week:
244	      - 1 landing page hypothesis to test
245	      - 5 questions to ask potential customers (and how to find them)
246	      - What a "green light" signal looks like vs. a "pivot" signal
247	      - How to validate willingness to pay without building anything
248	      - Time required: assume I have max 2 hours/day outside my job
249	
250	  - trigger: ":mvp"
251	    replace: |
252	      Validated idea: [IDEA]
253	      Core value proposition: [WHAT IT DOES FOR THE USER]
254	      My technical skills: Python, Bash, Linux sysadmin, some web
255	
256	      Design the smallest possible MVP:
257	      - The single feature that proves the core value
258	      - Tech stack recommendation (fast to build, easy to iterate)
259	      - What to cut ruthlessly from v1
260	      - How to get first 10 paying users
261	      - Milestones for weeks 1, 2, 4
262	
263	  - trigger: ":side-schedule"
264	    replace: |
265	      My job hours: [e.g. 9-6]
266	      Energy pattern: [morning person / evening person]
267	      Hours per week for side project: [X]
268	      Current project stage: [IDEA / VALIDATING / BUILDING / LAUNCHING]
269	
270	      Build me a realistic weekly schedule that protects my job performance
271	      while making consistent progress on the side project.
272	      Include: deep work blocks, admin tasks, learning slots, and recovery.
273	      Be brutally honest about what's achievable.
274	
275	  - trigger: ":legal-check"
276	    replace: |
277	      I'm employed at a company in the Netherlands and want to start a side business.
278	      My side project idea: [IDEA]
279	
280	      What do I need to know about:
281	      - Dutch employment law on side businesses (nevenactiviteiten)
282	      - Conflict of interest risks
283	      - IP ownership — what belongs to my employer vs. me
284	      - KVK registration basics for a ZZP/BV
285	      - Tax implications of earning side income in NL
286	
287	      Flag what I should verify with a lawyer vs. what's standard.
288	
289	  - trigger: ":pricing"
290	    replace: |
291	      Product/service: [DESCRIPTION]
292	      Target customer: [WHO]
293	      Competitors and their pricing: [IF KNOWN]
294	
295	      Help me set pricing strategy:
296	      - 3 pricing models that fit this type of product
297	      - Psychological pricing tactics relevant here
298	      - What "too cheap" signals vs. "too expensive" kills
299	      - Recommended price point for MVP launch and why
300	      - How to test pricing without committing permanently
301	
302	
303	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
304	  # 🐍 PYTHON
305	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
306	
307	  - trigger: ":py-explain"
308	    replace: |
309	      Python concept I don't understand: [CONCEPT]
310	
311	      Explain it to me like I'm a sysadmin who knows Bash and PowerShell well.
312	      Use analogies to things I already know.
313	      Show: simple example → real-world use case → common mistake to avoid.
314	      Max 3 code blocks. Keep it practical.
315	
316	  - trigger: ":py-review"
317	    replace: |
318	      Review this Python code:
319	
320	      [PASTE CODE HERE]
321	
322	      Tell me:
323	      1. Bugs or logic errors
324	      2. Non-pythonic patterns (and the idiomatic way)
325	      3. Performance issues
326	      4. Security concerns (if any)
327	      5. Refactored version with brief comments on changes
328	
329	      I'm learning, so explain the "why" behind each suggestion.
330	
331	  - trigger: ":py-build"
332	    replace: |
333	      I want to build: [DESCRIPTION OF SCRIPT/TOOL]
334	      Input: [WHAT IT RECEIVES]
335	      Output: [WHAT IT SHOULD PRODUCE]
336	      My level: beginner-intermediate Python, strong in Bash/sysadmin
337	
338	      Give me:
339	      - Recommended approach and libraries
340	      - Step-by-step breakdown before writing any code
341	      - Full working script with comments
342	      - How to test it and edge cases to handle
343	
344	  - trigger: ":py-debug"
345	    replace: |
346	      This Python code is failing:
347	
348	      [PASTE CODE]
349	
350	      Error message: [PASTE ERROR]
351	
352	      What I've already tried: [IF ANYTHING]
353	
354	      Find the root cause, explain why it happens, and give me the fix.
355	      Also tell me how to avoid this class of error in the future.
356	
357	  - trigger: ":py-automate"
358	    replace: |
359	      Repetitive task I do manually: [DESCRIBE THE TASK]
360	      Current approach: [BASH SCRIPT / MANUAL / EXCEL / etc]
361	      Environment: [WINDOWS SERVER / LINUX / BOTH]
362	
363	      Build me a Python script to automate this.
364	      Prioritize: readability, error handling, and easy modification later.
365	      Add a --dry-run flag if it involves file operations or system changes.
366	
367	  - trigger: ":py-concept"
368	    replace: |
369	      I want to understand this Python topic deeply: [TOPIC]
370	      e.g. decorators / generators / async / OOP / list comprehensions
371	
372	      Give me:
373	      - Conceptual explanation (no code yet)
374	      - When you actually need this vs. when it's overkill
375	      - 3 progressively complex examples
376	      - 1 real-world project where this shines
377	      - Most common misunderstanding about this topic
378	
379	
380	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
381	  # 🖥️ SYSADMIN & IT
382	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
383	
384	  - trigger: ":incident"
385	    replace: |
386	      Incident description: [WHAT IS BROKEN]
387	      Environment: [OS / SERVICE / INFRA — e.g. Proxmox / Linux / Windows Server]
388	      Error or symptom: [PASTE LOGS OR DESCRIBE]
389	      What I've tried: [STEPS ALREADY TAKEN]
390	
391	      Walk me through structured troubleshooting:
392	      1. Most likely root causes (ranked by probability)
393	      2. Diagnostic commands to run right now
394	      3. Step-by-step resolution path
395	      4. How to prevent recurrence
396	      5. What to document after resolving
397	
398	  - trigger: ":security-audit"
399	    replace: |
400	      System/service to audit: [DESCRIPTION]
401	      Environment: [OS / NETWORK SETUP]
402	
403	      Give me a security checklist covering:
404	      - Authentication and access control weaknesses
405	      - Network exposure and attack surface
406	      - Logging and monitoring gaps
407	      - Patch and vulnerability management
408	      - Quick wins (fix today) vs. long-term hardening
409	
410	      Format as actionable checklist with priority: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM
411	
412	  - trigger: ":runbook"
413	    replace: |
414	      Task or procedure: [DESCRIBE THE OPERATIONAL TASK]
415	      Environment: [INFRA DETAILS]
416	      Audience: [YOURSELF / JUNIOR COLLEAGUE / TEAM]
417	
418	      Write a complete runbook:
419	      - Purpose and scope
420	      - Prerequisites and access needed
421	      - Step-by-step procedure (numbered, with commands)
422	      - Expected output at each step
423	      - Rollback procedure if something goes wrong
424	      - Verification that it worked
425	
426	  - trigger: ":zabbix"
427	    replace: |
428	      I need to monitor: [SERVICE / METRIC / HOST]
429	      Current setup: Zabbix on Linux
430	
431	      Give me:
432	      - Recommended items/triggers to set up
433	      - Thresholds that make sense (with reasoning)
434	      - Alert escalation logic
435	      - Any Zabbix template I should use or adapt
436	      - Sample trigger expression if applicable
437	
438	  - trigger: ":proxmox"
439	    replace: |
440	      Proxmox task: [DESCRIBE WHAT YOU WANT TO DO]
441	      Current setup: [NUMBER OF NODES / STORAGE TYPE / NETWORK CONFIG]
442	
443	      Give me step-by-step instructions with:
444	      - CLI commands (preferred) and GUI alternative
445	      - What could go wrong and how to avoid it
446	      - How to verify it worked
447	      - Any performance or resource considerations
448	
449	  - trigger: ":network-debug"
450	    replace: |
451	      Network problem: [DESCRIBE THE ISSUE]
452	      Topology: [VLAN setup / switches / firewall / etc]
453	
454	      Give me a systematic debug approach:
455	      - Layer-by-layer diagnosis (L1 → L7)
456	      - Specific commands to run at each layer
457	      - Most likely culprits given the symptoms
458	      - Fix + how to document the resolution
459	
460	  - trigger: ":explain-tech"
461	    replace: |
462	      Technical concept to explain: [CONCEPT]
463	      Audience: [MANAGER / NON-TECHNICAL COLLEAGUE / JUNIOR]
464	
465	      Write a clear explanation with:
466	      - 1 analogy a non-tech person would understand
467	      - Why it matters in practical terms
468	      - What could go wrong if ignored
469	      - Max 150 words — be concise
470	
471	
472	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
473	  # 🎯 THINKING & DECISIONS
474	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
475	
476	  - trigger: ":decide"
477	    replace: |
478	      I need to make a decision about: [DECISION]
479	      Options I'm considering: [OPTION A] vs [OPTION B]
480	
481	      Apply the following frameworks:
482	      1. First principles thinking — break it down to fundamentals
483	      2. 10/10/10 rule — how will I feel in 10 minutes, 10 months, 10 years?
484	      3. Inversion — what's the worst that happens with each option?
485	
486	      Give me a clear recommendation at the end.
487	
488	  - trigger: ":brainstorm"
489	    replace: |
490	      Topic: [TOPIC]
491	
492	      Give me 15 unconventional ideas. No obvious ones.
493	      For each: one sentence explanation + why it's non-obvious.
494	      Push the boundaries. Think like a contrarian.
495	
496	  - trigger: ":challenge"
497	    replace: |
498	      I believe: [BELIEF OR ASSUMPTION]
499	
500	      Act as a sharp critic. Challenge this belief aggressively.
501	      Give me: 3 strong counterarguments, 2 blind spots I might have,
502	      and 1 question that would make me reconsider everything.
503	
504	  - trigger: ":reframe"
505	    replace: |
506	      Problem I'm facing: [PROBLEM]
507	
508	      Reframe this problem in 5 completely different ways.
509	      For each reframe, suggest one unconventional approach to solve it.
510	      The goal is to make me see this from an angle I haven't considered.
511	
512	  - trigger: ":stuck"
513	    replace: |
514	      I'm procrastinating on: [TASK]
515	      The real reason I think I'm avoiding it: [HONEST ANSWER]
516	
517	      Give me:
518	      - The psychological reason I'm stuck (be direct)
519	      - A 2-minute starter action to break the block right now
520	      - How to structure the next 25 minutes to make real progress
521	
522	
523	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
524	  # 💼 BUSINESS
525	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
526	
527	  - trigger: ":bizidea"
528	    replace: |
529	      Evaluate this business idea: [IDEA]
530	
531	      Give me:
532	      - Target market and real pain point it solves
533	      - 3 ways to validate it in under 2 weeks with $0
534	      - Main risks and how to mitigate them
535	      - Realistic revenue model
536	      - Similar existing businesses (so I can study them)
537	
538	  - trigger: ":competitor"
539	    replace: |
540	      Company/product to analyze: [NAME]
541	
542	      Break down:
543	      - Business model (how they actually make money)
544	      - Their biggest weaknesses and gaps
545	      - What users complain about most
546	      - 3 opportunities someone could exploit against them
547	      - Key metrics to track their growth
548	
549	  - trigger: ":gtm"
550	    replace: |
551	      Product: [PRODUCT]
552	      Target audience: [AUDIENCE]
553	      Budget: [BUDGET]
554	
555	      Create a go-to-market strategy for the first 90 days.
556	      Focus on: first 100 users, channels, messaging, and quick wins.
557	      Be specific — no generic advice.
558	
559	  - trigger: ":email-cold"
560	    replace: |
561	      I'm reaching out to: [PERSON/COMPANY]
562	      My goal: [WHAT I WANT]
563	      My offer/value: [WHAT I BRING]
564	
565	      Write a cold email. Max 5 lines. No fluff, no "I hope this finds you well".
566	      Subject line + body. Make it feel human, not templated.
567	
568	  - trigger: ":trends"
569	    replace: |
570	      Industry/field: [FIELD]
571	
572	      What are the 5 most underrated trends in this space right now?
573	      Not the mainstream ones everyone talks about — the ones that will matter
574	      in 2-3 years. For each: why it matters + one person/company already betting on it.
575	
576	
577	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
578	  # 🧠 LEARNING
579	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
580	
581	  - trigger: ":learn"
582	    replace: |
583	      I want to learn: [SKILL/TOPIC]
584	      Current level: [ZERO / SOME BASICS / INTERMEDIATE]
585	      Time available: [X hours/week]
586	
587	      Give me:
588	      - The 20% of knowledge that gives 80% of results (Pareto)
589	      - A 4-week learning roadmap
590	      - Best free resources (YouTube channels, docs, communities)
591	      - The #1 mistake beginners make in this field
592	
593	  - trigger: ":summarize"
594	    replace: |
595	      [PASTE TEXT / ARTICLE / VIDEO TRANSCRIPT HERE]
596	
597	      Summarize this in:
598	      - 3 bullet points (key takeaways)
599	      - 1 sentence (the core idea)
600	      - 1 action I can take today based on this
601	
602	  - trigger: ":rabbit-hole"
603	    replace: |
604	      I'm curious about: [TOPIC]
605	
606	      Take me down the rabbit hole. Give me:
607	      - The surface-level explanation (1 min read)
608	      - The deeper, non-obvious truth most people miss
609	      - 3 books, 2 YouTube channels, 1 subreddit to go deep
610	      - 1 mind-blowing fact that will make me want to explore more
611	
612	  - trigger: ":inspire"
613	    replace: |
614	      I'm feeling stuck creatively. Topic/field: [TOPIC]
615	
616	      Give me:
617	      - 3 websites or resources I've probably never heard of for inspiration
618	      - 2 creators (any medium) who think differently about this
619	      - 1 creative constraint or challenge to unstick me right now
620	
621	
622	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
623	  # 🎬 MOVIES & SERIES
624	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
625	
626	  - trigger: ":watchnext"
627	    replace: |
628	      I recently loved: [MOVIE/SERIES]
629	      What I liked most: [ACTING / PLOT / ATMOSPHERE / VISUALS / etc]
630	      Mood right now: [INTENSE / LIGHT / MINDBLOWING / EMOTIONAL / etc]
631	
632	      Recommend 5 titles I probably haven't seen.
633	      For each: 2-sentence pitch and why it matches my taste.
634	      No mainstream obvious picks.
635	
636	  - trigger: ":analyze-film"
637	    replace: |
638	      Film/Series: [TITLE]
639	
640	      Give me a deep analysis:
641	      - Core themes and what the director was really saying
642	      - Symbolism and recurring motifs
643	      - Why it resonates (or doesn't) culturally
644	      - Most underrated scene and why it matters
645	      - What to watch next if I loved this
646	
647	
648	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
649	  # 💪 LIFESTYLE
650	  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
651	
652	  - trigger: ":workout"
653	    replace: |
654	      My goal: [LOSE FAT / BUILD MUSCLE / IMPROVE ENDURANCE / etc]
655	      Training days per week: [NUMBER]
656	      Equipment available: [GYM / HOME / CROSSFIT BOX / etc]
657	      Current level: [BEGINNER / INTERMEDIATE / ADVANCED]
658	
659	      Build me a weekly training plan. Include sets, reps, rest times.
660	      Add a note on progressive overload strategy.
661	
662	  - trigger: ":nutrition"
663	    replace: |
664	      My goal: [CUT / BULK / MAINTAIN / PERFORMANCE]
665	      Weight: [KG] — Activity level: [SEDENTARY / ACTIVE / VERY ACTIVE]
666	      Dietary restrictions: [NONE / LACTOSE FREE / etc]
667	
668	      Give me: daily calorie target, macro split (protein/carbs/fat in grams),
669	      3 easy high-protein meal ideas, and 2 things to avoid.
670	
671	  - trigger: ":recover"
672	    replace: |
673	      I'm experiencing: [MUSCLE SORENESS / FATIGUE / JOINT PAIN / POOR SLEEP / etc]
674	      Training frequency: [X days/week]
675	
676	      Give me a science-backed recovery protocol for this week.
677	      Include: sleep optimization, nutrition timing, mobility work, and mental recovery.
678	
679	  - trigger: ":trip"
680	    replace: |
681	      Destination: [CITY/COUNTRY]
682	      Duration: [X days]
683	      Travel style: [BACKPACKER / COMFORT / LUXURY / LOCAL EXPERIENCE]
684	      Interests: [FOOD / ARCHITECTURE / NIGHTLIFE / NATURE / etc]
685	
686	      Build me a day-by-day itinerary. Include:
687	      - Hidden gems locals actually go to
688	      - What to skip (tourist traps)
689	      - Best neighborhoods to stay in
690	      - 3 food spots that are not on TripAdvisor top 10
691	
692	  - trigger: ":weekend"
693	    replace: |
694	      I'm based in: Netherlands (Ede/Amsterdam area)
695	      Budget: [LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH]
696	      I want to feel: [ADVENTUROUS / RELAXED / INSPIRED / SOCIAL]
697	
698	      Give me 5 weekend ideas I can do from here.
699	      Mix: local hidden spots + short train/flight trips in Europe.
700	
701	  - trigger: ":plan-week"
702	    replace: |
703	      My main goal this week: [GOAL]
704	      Hours available outside work: [X hours]
705	      Energy levels tend to be: [HIGH MORNING / HIGH EVENING / CONSISTENT]
706	
707	      Build me a realistic weekly plan. Block time for deep work, content creation,
708	      fitness, and recovery. Be honest about what's actually achievable.

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 🗡️  SWISS KNIFE — AI PROMPT DECK
# Florian Muntean · @techbyflo
# Last updated: 2026-03-30
#
# HOW TO USE:
#   · Type any trigger in any app and it expands automatically
#   · Alt+Space → search bar to find triggers without memorizing
#   · Replace ALL [PLACEHOLDERS] before sending to AI
#   · Best used with: Perplexity, ChatGPT, Claude
#
# TRIGGER INDEX:
#   🔬 Research      :research · :research-market · :research-person · :verify · :deep-research
#   🧱 Understanding :first-principles · :start-zero · :learn-sticky
#   🏗️  Side Business :validate · :mvp · :side-schedule · :legal-check · :pricing
#   🐍 Python        :py-explain · :py-review · :py-build · :py-debug · :py-automate · :py-concept
#   🖥️  Sysadmin      :incident · :security-audit · :runbook · :zabbix · :proxmox · :network-debug · :explain-tech
#   🎯 Thinking      :decide · :brainstorm · :challenge · :reframe · :stuck
#   💼 Business      :bizidea · :competitor · :gtm · :email-cold · :trends
#   🧠 Learning      :learn · :summarize · :rabbit-hole · :inspire
#   🎬 Entertainment :watchnext · :analyze-film
#   💪 Lifestyle     :workout · :nutrition · :recover · :trip · :weekend · :plan-week
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

matches:


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🔬 RESEARCH
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":research"
    replace: |
      Topic I want to research deeply: [TOPIC]

      Structure a complete research brief for me:
      1. Core question I'm really trying to answer
      2. Key subtopics to investigate (with priority order)
      3. Best primary sources (papers, reports, databases)
      4. Common misconceptions to avoid
      5. Experts/thought leaders worth following
      6. Open questions that even experts disagree on
      7. What I should believe by default vs. what needs verification

      Be exhaustive. I want to understand this at an expert level.

  - trigger: ":research-market"
    replace: |
      Market to research: [MARKET/INDUSTRY]

      Give me a structured market analysis:
      - Market size (TAM/SAM/SOM) and growth rate
      - Top 5 players and their market share
      - Key customer segments and their specific pain points
      - Main distribution channels
      - Regulatory or technical barriers to entry
      - Where money is flowing (VCs, acquisitions, trends)
      - Underserved niches with real potential

      Cite specific numbers where possible. Flag where data is uncertain.

  - trigger: ":research-person"
    replace: |
      Person to research: [NAME]
      Context: [WHY I'M RESEARCHING THEM — investor / potential partner / competitor / etc]

      Give me:
      - Career trajectory and key milestones
      - Their core beliefs and decision-making patterns
      - Public failures and what they learned
      - Best interviews, talks, or writings to consume
      - How they think about [RELEVANT TOPIC]
      - What they value most (based on their actions, not words)

  - trigger: ":verify"
    replace: |
      Claim I want to verify: [CLAIM]
      Source it came from: [SOURCE]

      Help me evaluate this claim:
      - Is there strong evidence for or against it?
      - What do contrarian credible sources say?
      - What's the confidence level I should assign (high/medium/low)?
      - What would I need to find to fully confirm or debunk this?

  - trigger: ":deep-research"
    replace: |
      Do deep research on trends on topic: [TOPIC]

      Step 1 — Research
      Search broadly across: industry reports, academic sources, news, forums,
      investor activity, and expert opinion. Collect at least 10 distinct trends.
      For each trend note: evidence strength, recency, and source type.

      Step 2 — Cross-reference & filter
      Analyze all trends found. Identify which ones:
      - Appear across multiple independent sources
      - Have growing momentum (not peaking or declining)
      - Have the highest impact potential for [TARGET CONTEXT: business / tech / market]
      Rank them and select the top 3 with clear justification for why the others were cut.

      Step 3 — One-page memo
      Draft a concise executive memo with this structure:

      SUBJECT: Top 3 Emerging Trends in [TOPIC]
      DATE: {{mydate}}

      OVERVIEW (2-3 sentences on why this topic matters now)

      TREND 1: [NAME]
      - What it is
      - Evidence & sources
      - Why it matters & opportunity/risk

      TREND 2: [NAME]
      - What it is
      - Evidence & sources
      - Why it matters & opportunity/risk

      TREND 3: [NAME]
      - What it is
      - Evidence & sources
      - Why it matters & opportunity/risk

      BOTTOM LINE (1-2 sentences: what should someone do with this information)
    vars:
      - name: mydate
        type: date
        params:
          format: "%Y-%m-%d"


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🧱 UNDERSTANDING FROM SCRATCH
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":first-principles"
    replace: |
      I don't fully understand: [CONCEPT / PROBLEM / SYSTEM]
      My current understanding: [WHAT I THINK I KNOW, OR "NONE"]

      Break this down to absolute fundamentals using first principles thinking:

      LAYER 1 — Core question
      What is the single most fundamental question this concept answers?
      Strip away all assumptions. What is this really about at its core?

      LAYER 2 — Building blocks
      What are the 3-5 foundational pieces I must understand before anything else?
      Explain each one independently. No jargon. If you use a technical term, define it immediately.

      LAYER 3 — How the pieces connect
      Now show me how those building blocks interact to create the full concept.
      Use a simple diagram in text (ASCII or structured list) if helpful.

      LAYER 4 — Where people get confused
      What is the #1 misconception beginners have about this?
      Why does it feel confusing and what's the mental shift needed?

      LAYER 5 — Concrete grounding
      Give me one real-world example I can touch, see, or experience.
      Something from everyday life or from IT/sysadmin/tech that makes it click.

      End with: one question I should be able to answer if I've truly understood this.

  - trigger: ":start-zero"
    replace: |
      I want to start: [SKILL / PROJECT / TOPIC / TOOL]
      My current knowledge: ZERO (or close to it)
      My goal: [WHAT I WANT TO BE ABLE TO DO — be specific]
      Time I can invest: [X hours/week]

      I need a complete "from zero" starter guide:

      MINDSET FIRST
      What is the right mental model to approach this?
      What should I expect in the first 2 weeks (frustrations, progress, milestones)?

      THE MINIMUM VIABLE KNOWLEDGE
      What are the absolute minimum 5 things I need to understand to get started?
      Not everything — just what unlocks the first real progress.

      DAY 1 ACTION PLAN
      What do I do in the first 60 minutes? Be extremely specific.
      Not "read the docs" — tell me exactly what to open, what to type, what to produce.

      WEEK 1 ROADMAP
      Day-by-day breakdown. Small wins that build momentum.
      Each day: 1 concept + 1 hands-on task + 1 thing to produce/test.

      COMMON BEGINNER TRAPS
      Top 3 mistakes that waste time at the start.
      What to ignore completely for now (things that feel important but aren't yet).

      FIRST MILESTONE
      What does "I'm no longer a complete beginner" look like?
      Give me a concrete test or project that proves I've crossed that line.

  - trigger: ":learn-sticky"
    replace: |
      I want to understand and remember: [CONCEPT / TOPIC / SKILL]
      My background: sysadmin, know Linux/networking/Python basics, visual thinker
      Why I keep forgetting it: [IF YOU KNOW — "it's too abstract" / "too many terms" / etc]

      Make this stick in my long-term memory using these techniques:

      THE ANALOGY
      Find the best real-world analogy for this concept.
      It must come from: everyday life, cooking, architecture, sports, or infrastructure/IT.
      Explain the analogy fully, then map each part explicitly:
      "[Part A] in the analogy = [Part A] in the concept because..."

      THE VISUAL MENTAL MODEL
      Describe a vivid mental image or scene I can picture in my head.
      Something I can "replay" when I need to recall this concept.
      Make it slightly absurd or exaggerated — it sticks better.

      THE MNEMONIC
      Create an acronym, rhyme, or memorable phrase that encodes the key parts.
      If it's a process: give me the first letters of each step as a word.
      If it's a definition: give me a one-line phrase that sounds like the concept.

      THE STORY
      Turn this concept into a 5-sentence mini story with characters.
      The characters represent the components. The plot represents how they interact.
      Make it slightly dramatic or funny.

      THE QUICK TEST
      Give me 3 questions I can ask myself tomorrow to check if it stuck.
      One recall question, one application question, one "explain it to someone else" question.


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🏗️ BUILDING A COMPANY WHILE EMPLOYED
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":validate"
    replace: |
      Business idea: [IDEA]
      Target customer: [WHO]
      Assumed problem: [PROBLEM YOU THINK THEY HAVE]

      Design a lean validation plan for this week:
      - 1 landing page hypothesis to test
      - 5 questions to ask potential customers (and how to find them)
      - What a "green light" signal looks like vs. a "pivot" signal
      - How to validate willingness to pay without building anything
      - Time required: assume I have max 2 hours/day outside my job

  - trigger: ":mvp"
    replace: |
      Validated idea: [IDEA]
      Core value proposition: [WHAT IT DOES FOR THE USER]
      My technical skills: Python, Bash, Linux sysadmin, some web

      Design the smallest possible MVP:
      - The single feature that proves the core value
      - Tech stack recommendation (fast to build, easy to iterate)
      - What to cut ruthlessly from v1
      - How to get first 10 paying users
      - Milestones for weeks 1, 2, 4

  - trigger: ":side-schedule"
    replace: |
      My job hours: [e.g. 9-6]
      Energy pattern: [morning person / evening person]
      Hours per week for side project: [X]
      Current project stage: [IDEA / VALIDATING / BUILDING / LAUNCHING]

      Build me a realistic weekly schedule that protects my job performance
      while making consistent progress on the side project.
      Include: deep work blocks, admin tasks, learning slots, and recovery.
      Be brutally honest about what's achievable.

  - trigger: ":legal-check"
    replace: |
      I'm employed at a company in the Netherlands and want to start a side business.
      My side project idea: [IDEA]

      What do I need to know about:
      - Dutch employment law on side businesses (nevenactiviteiten)
      - Conflict of interest risks
      - IP ownership — what belongs to my employer vs. me
      - KVK registration basics for a ZZP/BV
      - Tax implications of earning side income in NL

      Flag what I should verify with a lawyer vs. what's standard.

  - trigger: ":pricing"
    replace: |
      Product/service: [DESCRIPTION]
      Target customer: [WHO]
      Competitors and their pricing: [IF KNOWN]

      Help me set pricing strategy:
      - 3 pricing models that fit this type of product
      - Psychological pricing tactics relevant here
      - What "too cheap" signals vs. "too expensive" kills
      - Recommended price point for MVP launch and why
      - How to test pricing without committing permanently


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🐍 PYTHON
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":py-explain"
    replace: |
      Python concept I don't understand: [CONCEPT]

      Explain it to me like I'm a sysadmin who knows Bash and PowerShell well.
      Use analogies to things I already know.
      Show: simple example → real-world use case → common mistake to avoid.
      Max 3 code blocks. Keep it practical.

  - trigger: ":py-review"
    replace: |
      Review this Python code:

      [PASTE CODE HERE]

      Tell me:
      1. Bugs or logic errors
      2. Non-pythonic patterns (and the idiomatic way)
      3. Performance issues
      4. Security concerns (if any)
      5. Refactored version with brief comments on changes

      I'm learning, so explain the "why" behind each suggestion.

  - trigger: ":py-build"
    replace: |
      I want to build: [DESCRIPTION OF SCRIPT/TOOL]
      Input: [WHAT IT RECEIVES]
      Output: [WHAT IT SHOULD PRODUCE]
      My level: beginner-intermediate Python, strong in Bash/sysadmin

      Give me:
      - Recommended approach and libraries
      - Step-by-step breakdown before writing any code
      - Full working script with comments
      - How to test it and edge cases to handle

  - trigger: ":py-debug"
    replace: |
      This Python code is failing:

      [PASTE CODE]

      Error message: [PASTE ERROR]

      What I've already tried: [IF ANYTHING]

      Find the root cause, explain why it happens, and give me the fix.
      Also tell me how to avoid this class of error in the future.

  - trigger: ":py-automate"
    replace: |
      Repetitive task I do manually: [DESCRIBE THE TASK]
      Current approach: [BASH SCRIPT / MANUAL / EXCEL / etc]
      Environment: [WINDOWS SERVER / LINUX / BOTH]

      Build me a Python script to automate this.
      Prioritize: readability, error handling, and easy modification later.
      Add a --dry-run flag if it involves file operations or system changes.

  - trigger: ":py-concept"
    replace: |
      I want to understand this Python topic deeply: [TOPIC]
      e.g. decorators / generators / async / OOP / list comprehensions

      Give me:
      - Conceptual explanation (no code yet)
      - When you actually need this vs. when it's overkill
      - 3 progressively complex examples
      - 1 real-world project where this shines
      - Most common misunderstanding about this topic


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🖥️ SYSADMIN & IT
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":incident"
    replace: |
      Incident description: [WHAT IS BROKEN]
      Environment: [OS / SERVICE / INFRA — e.g. Proxmox / Linux / Windows Server]
      Error or symptom: [PASTE LOGS OR DESCRIBE]
      What I've tried: [STEPS ALREADY TAKEN]

      Walk me through structured troubleshooting:
      1. Most likely root causes (ranked by probability)
      2. Diagnostic commands to run right now
      3. Step-by-step resolution path
      4. How to prevent recurrence
      5. What to document after resolving

  - trigger: ":security-audit"
    replace: |
      System/service to audit: [DESCRIPTION]
      Environment: [OS / NETWORK SETUP]

      Give me a security checklist covering:
      - Authentication and access control weaknesses
      - Network exposure and attack surface
      - Logging and monitoring gaps
      - Patch and vulnerability management
      - Quick wins (fix today) vs. long-term hardening

      Format as actionable checklist with priority: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM

  - trigger: ":runbook"
    replace: |
      Task or procedure: [DESCRIBE THE OPERATIONAL TASK]
      Environment: [INFRA DETAILS]
      Audience: [YOURSELF / JUNIOR COLLEAGUE / TEAM]

      Write a complete runbook:
      - Purpose and scope
      - Prerequisites and access needed
      - Step-by-step procedure (numbered, with commands)
      - Expected output at each step
      - Rollback procedure if something goes wrong
      - Verification that it worked

  - trigger: ":zabbix"
    replace: |
      I need to monitor: [SERVICE / METRIC / HOST]
      Current setup: Zabbix on Linux

      Give me:
      - Recommended items/triggers to set up
      - Thresholds that make sense (with reasoning)
      - Alert escalation logic
      - Any Zabbix template I should use or adapt
      - Sample trigger expression if applicable

  - trigger: ":proxmox"
    replace: |
      Proxmox task: [DESCRIBE WHAT YOU WANT TO DO]
      Current setup: [NUMBER OF NODES / STORAGE TYPE / NETWORK CONFIG]

      Give me step-by-step instructions with:
      - CLI commands (preferred) and GUI alternative
      - What could go wrong and how to avoid it
      - How to verify it worked
      - Any performance or resource considerations

  - trigger: ":network-debug"
    replace: |
      Network problem: [DESCRIBE THE ISSUE]
      Topology: [VLAN setup / switches / firewall / etc]

      Give me a systematic debug approach:
      - Layer-by-layer diagnosis (L1 → L7)
      - Specific commands to run at each layer
      - Most likely culprits given the symptoms
      - Fix + how to document the resolution

  - trigger: ":explain-tech"
    replace: |
      Technical concept to explain: [CONCEPT]
      Audience: [MANAGER / NON-TECHNICAL COLLEAGUE / JUNIOR]

      Write a clear explanation with:
      - 1 analogy a non-tech person would understand
      - Why it matters in practical terms
      - What could go wrong if ignored
      - Max 150 words — be concise


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🎯 THINKING & DECISIONS
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":decide"
    replace: |
      I need to make a decision about: [DECISION]
      Options I'm considering: [OPTION A] vs [OPTION B]

      Apply the following frameworks:
      1. First principles thinking — break it down to fundamentals
      2. 10/10/10 rule — how will I feel in 10 minutes, 10 months, 10 years?
      3. Inversion — what's the worst that happens with each option?

      Give me a clear recommendation at the end.

  - trigger: ":brainstorm"
    replace: |
      Topic: [TOPIC]

      Give me 15 unconventional ideas. No obvious ones.
      For each: one sentence explanation + why it's non-obvious.
      Push the boundaries. Think like a contrarian.

  - trigger: ":challenge"
    replace: |
      I believe: [BELIEF OR ASSUMPTION]

      Act as a sharp critic. Challenge this belief aggressively.
      Give me: 3 strong counterarguments, 2 blind spots I might have,
      and 1 question that would make me reconsider everything.

  - trigger: ":reframe"
    replace: |
      Problem I'm facing: [PROBLEM]

      Reframe this problem in 5 completely different ways.
      For each reframe, suggest one unconventional approach to solve it.
      The goal is to make me see this from an angle I haven't considered.

  - trigger: ":stuck"
    replace: |
      I'm procrastinating on: [TASK]
      The real reason I think I'm avoiding it: [HONEST ANSWER]

      Give me:
      - The psychological reason I'm stuck (be direct)
      - A 2-minute starter action to break the block right now
      - How to structure the next 25 minutes to make real progress


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 💼 BUSINESS
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":bizidea"
    replace: |
      Evaluate this business idea: [IDEA]

      Give me:
      - Target market and real pain point it solves
      - 3 ways to validate it in under 2 weeks with $0
      - Main risks and how to mitigate them
      - Realistic revenue model
      - Similar existing businesses (so I can study them)

  - trigger: ":competitor"
    replace: |
      Company/product to analyze: [NAME]

      Break down:
      - Business model (how they actually make money)
      - Their biggest weaknesses and gaps
      - What users complain about most
      - 3 opportunities someone could exploit against them
      - Key metrics to track their growth

  - trigger: ":gtm"
    replace: |
      Product: [PRODUCT]
      Target audience: [AUDIENCE]
      Budget: [BUDGET]

      Create a go-to-market strategy for the first 90 days.
      Focus on: first 100 users, channels, messaging, and quick wins.
      Be specific — no generic advice.

  - trigger: ":email-cold"
    replace: |
      I'm reaching out to: [PERSON/COMPANY]
      My goal: [WHAT I WANT]
      My offer/value: [WHAT I BRING]

      Write a cold email. Max 5 lines. No fluff, no "I hope this finds you well".
      Subject line + body. Make it feel human, not templated.

  - trigger: ":trends"
    replace: |
      Industry/field: [FIELD]

      What are the 5 most underrated trends in this space right now?
      Not the mainstream ones everyone talks about — the ones that will matter
      in 2-3 years. For each: why it matters + one person/company already betting on it.


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🧠 LEARNING
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":learn"
    replace: |
      I want to learn: [SKILL/TOPIC]
      Current level: [ZERO / SOME BASICS / INTERMEDIATE]
      Time available: [X hours/week]

      Give me:
      - The 20% of knowledge that gives 80% of results (Pareto)
      - A 4-week learning roadmap
      - Best free resources (YouTube channels, docs, communities)
      - The #1 mistake beginners make in this field

  - trigger: ":summarize"
    replace: |
      [PASTE TEXT / ARTICLE / VIDEO TRANSCRIPT HERE]

      Summarize this in:
      - 3 bullet points (key takeaways)
      - 1 sentence (the core idea)
      - 1 action I can take today based on this

  - trigger: ":rabbit-hole"
    replace: |
      I'm curious about: [TOPIC]

      Take me down the rabbit hole. Give me:
      - The surface-level explanation (1 min read)
      - The deeper, non-obvious truth most people miss
      - 3 books, 2 YouTube channels, 1 subreddit to go deep
      - 1 mind-blowing fact that will make me want to explore more

  - trigger: ":inspire"
    replace: |
      I'm feeling stuck creatively. Topic/field: [TOPIC]

      Give me:
      - 3 websites or resources I've probably never heard of for inspiration
      - 2 creators (any medium) who think differently about this
      - 1 creative constraint or challenge to unstick me right now


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🎬 MOVIES & SERIES
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":watchnext"
    replace: |
      I recently loved: [MOVIE/SERIES]
      What I liked most: [ACTING / PLOT / ATMOSPHERE / VISUALS / etc]
      Mood right now: [INTENSE / LIGHT / MINDBLOWING / EMOTIONAL / etc]

      Recommend 5 titles I probably haven't seen.
      For each: 2-sentence pitch and why it matches my taste.
      No mainstream obvious picks.

  - trigger: ":analyze-film"
    replace: |
      Film/Series: [TITLE]

      Give me a deep analysis:
      - Core themes and what the director was really saying
      - Symbolism and recurring motifs
      - Why it resonates (or doesn't) culturally
      - Most underrated scene and why it matters
      - What to watch next if I loved this


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 💪 LIFESTYLE
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":workout"
    replace: |
      My goal: [LOSE FAT / BUILD MUSCLE / IMPROVE ENDURANCE / etc]
      Training days per week: [NUMBER]
      Equipment available: [GYM / HOME / CROSSFIT BOX / etc]
      Current level: [BEGINNER / INTERMEDIATE / ADVANCED]

      Build me a weekly training plan. Include sets, reps, rest times.
      Add a note on progressive overload strategy.

  - trigger: ":nutrition"
    replace: |
      My goal: [CUT / BULK / MAINTAIN / PERFORMANCE]
      Weight: [KG] — Activity level: [SEDENTARY / ACTIVE / VERY ACTIVE]
      Dietary restrictions: [NONE / LACTOSE FREE / etc]

      Give me: daily calorie target, macro split (protein/carbs/fat in grams),
      3 easy high-protein meal ideas, and 2 things to avoid.

  - trigger: ":recover"
    replace: |
      I'm experiencing: [MUSCLE SORENESS / FATIGUE / JOINT PAIN / POOR SLEEP / etc]
      Training frequency: [X days/week]

      Give me a science-backed recovery protocol for this week.
      Include: sleep optimization, nutrition timing, mobility work, and mental recovery.

  - trigger: ":trip"
    replace: |
      Destination: [CITY/COUNTRY]
      Duration: [X days]
      Travel style: [BACKPACKER / COMFORT / LUXURY / LOCAL EXPERIENCE]
      Interests: [FOOD / ARCHITECTURE / NIGHTLIFE / NATURE / etc]

      Build me a day-by-day itinerary. Include:
      - Hidden gems locals actually go to
      - What to skip (tourist traps)
      - Best neighborhoods to stay in
      - 3 food spots that are not on TripAdvisor top 10

  - trigger: ":weekend"
    replace: |
      I'm based in: Netherlands (Ede/Amsterdam area)
      Budget: [LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH]
      I want to feel: [ADVENTUROUS / RELAXED / INSPIRED / SOCIAL]

      Give me 5 weekend ideas I can do from here.
      Mix: local hidden spots + short train/flight trips in Europe.

  - trigger: ":plan-week"
    replace: |
      My main goal this week: [GOAL]
      Hours available outside work: [X hours]
      Energy levels tend to be: [HIGH MORNING / HIGH EVENING / CONSISTENT]

      Build me a realistic weekly plan. Block time for deep work, content creation,
      fitness, and recovery. Be honest about what's actually achievable.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 🗡️  SWISS KNIFE — AI PROMPT DECK
# Florian Muntean · @techbyflo
# Last updated: 2026-03-30
#
# HOW TO USE:
#   · Type any trigger in any app and it expands automatically
#   · Alt+Space → search bar to find triggers without memorizing
#   · Replace ALL [PLACEHOLDERS] before sending to AI
#   · Best used with: Perplexity, ChatGPT, Claude
#
# TRIGGER INDEX:
#   🔬 Research      :research · :research-market · :research-person · :verify · :deep-research
#   🧱 Understanding :first-principles · :start-zero · :learn-sticky
#   🏗️  Side Business :validate · :mvp · :side-schedule · :legal-check · :pricing
#   🐍 Python        :py-explain · :py-review · :py-build · :py-debug · :py-automate · :py-concept
#   🖥️  Sysadmin      :incident · :security-audit · :runbook · :zabbix · :proxmox · :network-debug · :explain-tech
#   🎯 Thinking      :decide · :brainstorm · :challenge · :reframe · :stuck
#   💼 Business      :bizidea · :competitor · :gtm · :email-cold · :trends
#   🧠 Learning      :learn · :summarize · :rabbit-hole · :inspire
#   🎬 Entertainment :watchnext · :analyze-film
#   💪 Lifestyle     :workout · :nutrition · :recover · :trip · :weekend · :plan-week
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

matches:


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🔬 RESEARCH
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":research"
    replace: |
      Topic I want to research deeply: [TOPIC]

      Structure a complete research brief for me:
      1. Core question I'm really trying to answer
      2. Key subtopics to investigate (with priority order)
      3. Best primary sources (papers, reports, databases)
      4. Common misconceptions to avoid
      5. Experts/thought leaders worth following
      6. Open questions that even experts disagree on
      7. What I should believe by default vs. what needs verification

      Be exhaustive. I want to understand this at an expert level.

  - trigger: ":research-market"
    replace: |
      Market to research: [MARKET/INDUSTRY]

      Give me a structured market analysis:
      - Market size (TAM/SAM/SOM) and growth rate
      - Top 5 players and their market share
      - Key customer segments and their specific pain points
      - Main distribution channels
      - Regulatory or technical barriers to entry
      - Where money is flowing (VCs, acquisitions, trends)
      - Underserved niches with real potential

      Cite specific numbers where possible. Flag where data is uncertain.

  - trigger: ":research-person"
    replace: |
      Person to research: [NAME]
      Context: [WHY I'M RESEARCHING THEM — investor / potential partner / competitor / etc]

      Give me:
      - Career trajectory and key milestones
      - Their core beliefs and decision-making patterns
      - Public failures and what they learned
      - Best interviews, talks, or writings to consume
      - How they think about [RELEVANT TOPIC]
      - What they value most (based on their actions, not words)

  - trigger: ":verify"
    replace: |
      Claim I want to verify: [CLAIM]
      Source it came from: [SOURCE]

      Help me evaluate this claim:
      - Is there strong evidence for or against it?
      - What do contrarian credible sources say?
      - What's the confidence level I should assign (high/medium/low)?
      - What would I need to find to fully confirm or debunk this?

  - trigger: ":deep-research"
    replace: |
      Do deep research on trends on topic: [TOPIC]

      Step 1 — Research
      Search broadly across: industry reports, academic sources, news, forums,
      investor activity, and expert opinion. Collect at least 10 distinct trends.
      For each trend note: evidence strength, recency, and source type.

      Step 2 — Cross-reference & filter
      Analyze all trends found. Identify which ones:
      - Appear across multiple independent sources
      - Have growing momentum (not peaking or declining)
      - Have the highest impact potential for [TARGET CONTEXT: business / tech / market]
      Rank them and select the top 3 with clear justification for why the others were cut.

      Step 3 — One-page memo
      Draft a concise executive memo with this structure:

      SUBJECT: Top 3 Emerging Trends in [TOPIC]
      DATE: {{mydate}}

      OVERVIEW (2-3 sentences on why this topic matters now)

      TREND 1: [NAME]
      - What it is
      - Evidence & sources
      - Why it matters & opportunity/risk

      TREND 2: [NAME]
      - What it is
      - Evidence & sources
      - Why it matters & opportunity/risk

      TREND 3: [NAME]
      - What it is
      - Evidence & sources
      - Why it matters & opportunity/risk

      BOTTOM LINE (1-2 sentences: what should someone do with this information)
    vars:
      - name: mydate
        type: date
        params:
          format: "%Y-%m-%d"


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🧱 UNDERSTANDING FROM SCRATCH
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":first-principles"
    replace: |
      I don't fully understand: [CONCEPT / PROBLEM / SYSTEM]
      My current understanding: [WHAT I THINK I KNOW, OR "NONE"]

      Break this down to absolute fundamentals using first principles thinking:

      LAYER 1 — Core question
      What is the single most fundamental question this concept answers?
      Strip away all assumptions. What is this really about at its core?

      LAYER 2 — Building blocks
      What are the 3-5 foundational pieces I must understand before anything else?
      Explain each one independently. No jargon. If you use a technical term, define it immediately.

      LAYER 3 — How the pieces connect
      Now show me how those building blocks interact to create the full concept.
      Use a simple diagram in text (ASCII or structured list) if helpful.

      LAYER 4 — Where people get confused
      What is the #1 misconception beginners have about this?
      Why does it feel confusing and what's the mental shift needed?

      LAYER 5 — Concrete grounding
      Give me one real-world example I can touch, see, or experience.
      Something from everyday life or from IT/sysadmin/tech that makes it click.

      End with: one question I should be able to answer if I've truly understood this.

  - trigger: ":start-zero"
    replace: |
      I want to start: [SKILL / PROJECT / TOPIC / TOOL]
      My current knowledge: ZERO (or close to it)
      My goal: [WHAT I WANT TO BE ABLE TO DO — be specific]
      Time I can invest: [X hours/week]

      I need a complete "from zero" starter guide:

      MINDSET FIRST
      What is the right mental model to approach this?
      What should I expect in the first 2 weeks (frustrations, progress, milestones)?

      THE MINIMUM VIABLE KNOWLEDGE
      What are the absolute minimum 5 things I need to understand to get started?
      Not everything — just what unlocks the first real progress.

      DAY 1 ACTION PLAN
      What do I do in the first 60 minutes? Be extremely specific.
      Not "read the docs" — tell me exactly what to open, what to type, what to produce.

      WEEK 1 ROADMAP
      Day-by-day breakdown. Small wins that build momentum.
      Each day: 1 concept + 1 hands-on task + 1 thing to produce/test.

      COMMON BEGINNER TRAPS
      Top 3 mistakes that waste time at the start.
      What to ignore completely for now (things that feel important but aren't yet).

      FIRST MILESTONE
      What does "I'm no longer a complete beginner" look like?
      Give me a concrete test or project that proves I've crossed that line.

  - trigger: ":learn-sticky"
    replace: |
      I want to understand and remember: [CONCEPT / TOPIC / SKILL]
      My background: sysadmin, know Linux/networking/Python basics, visual thinker
      Why I keep forgetting it: [IF YOU KNOW — "it's too abstract" / "too many terms" / etc]

      Make this stick in my long-term memory using these techniques:

      THE ANALOGY
      Find the best real-world analogy for this concept.
      It must come from: everyday life, cooking, architecture, sports, or infrastructure/IT.
      Explain the analogy fully, then map each part explicitly:
      "[Part A] in the analogy = [Part A] in the concept because..."

      THE VISUAL MENTAL MODEL
      Describe a vivid mental image or scene I can picture in my head.
      Something I can "replay" when I need to recall this concept.
      Make it slightly absurd or exaggerated — it sticks better.

      THE MNEMONIC
      Create an acronym, rhyme, or memorable phrase that encodes the key parts.
      If it's a process: give me the first letters of each step as a word.
      If it's a definition: give me a one-line phrase that sounds like the concept.

      THE STORY
      Turn this concept into a 5-sentence mini story with characters.
      The characters represent the components. The plot represents how they interact.
      Make it slightly dramatic or funny.

      THE QUICK TEST
      Give me 3 questions I can ask myself tomorrow to check if it stuck.
      One recall question, one application question, one "explain it to someone else" question.


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🏗️ BUILDING A COMPANY WHILE EMPLOYED
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":validate"
    replace: |
      Business idea: [IDEA]
      Target customer: [WHO]
      Assumed problem: [PROBLEM YOU THINK THEY HAVE]

      Design a lean validation plan for this week:
      - 1 landing page hypothesis to test
      - 5 questions to ask potential customers (and how to find them)
      - What a "green light" signal looks like vs. a "pivot" signal
      - How to validate willingness to pay without building anything
      - Time required: assume I have max 2 hours/day outside my job

  - trigger: ":mvp"
    replace: |
      Validated idea: [IDEA]
      Core value proposition: [WHAT IT DOES FOR THE USER]
      My technical skills: Python, Bash, Linux sysadmin, some web

      Design the smallest possible MVP:
      - The single feature that proves the core value
      - Tech stack recommendation (fast to build, easy to iterate)
      - What to cut ruthlessly from v1
      - How to get first 10 paying users
      - Milestones for weeks 1, 2, 4

  - trigger: ":side-schedule"
    replace: |
      My job hours: [e.g. 9-6]
      Energy pattern: [morning person / evening person]
      Hours per week for side project: [X]
      Current project stage: [IDEA / VALIDATING / BUILDING / LAUNCHING]

      Build me a realistic weekly schedule that protects my job performance
      while making consistent progress on the side project.
      Include: deep work blocks, admin tasks, learning slots, and recovery.
      Be brutally honest about what's achievable.

  - trigger: ":legal-check"
    replace: |
      I'm employed at a company in the Netherlands and want to start a side business.
      My side project idea: [IDEA]

      What do I need to know about:
      - Dutch employment law on side businesses (nevenactiviteiten)
      - Conflict of interest risks
      - IP ownership — what belongs to my employer vs. me
      - KVK registration basics for a ZZP/BV
      - Tax implications of earning side income in NL

      Flag what I should verify with a lawyer vs. what's standard.

  - trigger: ":pricing"
    replace: |
      Product/service: [DESCRIPTION]
      Target customer: [WHO]
      Competitors and their pricing: [IF KNOWN]

      Help me set pricing strategy:
      - 3 pricing models that fit this type of product
      - Psychological pricing tactics relevant here
      - What "too cheap" signals vs. "too expensive" kills
      - Recommended price point for MVP launch and why
      - How to test pricing without committing permanently


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🐍 PYTHON
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":py-explain"
    replace: |
      Python concept I don't understand: [CONCEPT]

      Explain it to me like I'm a sysadmin who knows Bash and PowerShell well.
      Use analogies to things I already know.
      Show: simple example → real-world use case → common mistake to avoid.
      Max 3 code blocks. Keep it practical.

  - trigger: ":py-review"
    replace: |
      Review this Python code:

      [PASTE CODE HERE]

      Tell me:
      1. Bugs or logic errors
      2. Non-pythonic patterns (and the idiomatic way)
      3. Performance issues
      4. Security concerns (if any)
      5. Refactored version with brief comments on changes

      I'm learning, so explain the "why" behind each suggestion.

  - trigger: ":py-build"
    replace: |
      I want to build: [DESCRIPTION OF SCRIPT/TOOL]
      Input: [WHAT IT RECEIVES]
      Output: [WHAT IT SHOULD PRODUCE]
      My level: beginner-intermediate Python, strong in Bash/sysadmin

      Give me:
      - Recommended approach and libraries
      - Step-by-step breakdown before writing any code
      - Full working script with comments
      - How to test it and edge cases to handle

  - trigger: ":py-debug"
    replace: |
      This Python code is failing:

      [PASTE CODE]

      Error message: [PASTE ERROR]

      What I've already tried: [IF ANYTHING]

      Find the root cause, explain why it happens, and give me the fix.
      Also tell me how to avoid this class of error in the future.

  - trigger: ":py-automate"
    replace: |
      Repetitive task I do manually: [DESCRIBE THE TASK]
      Current approach: [BASH SCRIPT / MANUAL / EXCEL / etc]
      Environment: [WINDOWS SERVER / LINUX / BOTH]

      Build me a Python script to automate this.
      Prioritize: readability, error handling, and easy modification later.
      Add a --dry-run flag if it involves file operations or system changes.

  - trigger: ":py-concept"
    replace: |
      I want to understand this Python topic deeply: [TOPIC]
      e.g. decorators / generators / async / OOP / list comprehensions

      Give me:
      - Conceptual explanation (no code yet)
      - When you actually need this vs. when it's overkill
      - 3 progressively complex examples
      - 1 real-world project where this shines
      - Most common misunderstanding about this topic


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🖥️ SYSADMIN & IT
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":incident"
    replace: |
      Incident description: [WHAT IS BROKEN]
      Environment: [OS / SERVICE / INFRA — e.g. Proxmox / Linux / Windows Server]
      Error or symptom: [PASTE LOGS OR DESCRIBE]
      What I've tried: [STEPS ALREADY TAKEN]

      Walk me through structured troubleshooting:
      1. Most likely root causes (ranked by probability)
      2. Diagnostic commands to run right now
      3. Step-by-step resolution path
      4. How to prevent recurrence
      5. What to document after resolving

  - trigger: ":security-audit"
    replace: |
      System/service to audit: [DESCRIPTION]
      Environment: [OS / NETWORK SETUP]

      Give me a security checklist covering:
      - Authentication and access control weaknesses
      - Network exposure and attack surface
      - Logging and monitoring gaps
      - Patch and vulnerability management
      - Quick wins (fix today) vs. long-term hardening

      Format as actionable checklist with priority: CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM

  - trigger: ":runbook"
    replace: |
      Task or procedure: [DESCRIBE THE OPERATIONAL TASK]
      Environment: [INFRA DETAILS]
      Audience: [YOURSELF / JUNIOR COLLEAGUE / TEAM]

      Write a complete runbook:
      - Purpose and scope
      - Prerequisites and access needed
      - Step-by-step procedure (numbered, with commands)
      - Expected output at each step
      - Rollback procedure if something goes wrong
      - Verification that it worked

  - trigger: ":zabbix"
    replace: |
      I need to monitor: [SERVICE / METRIC / HOST]
      Current setup: Zabbix on Linux

      Give me:
      - Recommended items/triggers to set up
      - Thresholds that make sense (with reasoning)
      - Alert escalation logic
      - Any Zabbix template I should use or adapt
      - Sample trigger expression if applicable

  - trigger: ":proxmox"
    replace: |
      Proxmox task: [DESCRIBE WHAT YOU WANT TO DO]
      Current setup: [NUMBER OF NODES / STORAGE TYPE / NETWORK CONFIG]

      Give me step-by-step instructions with:
      - CLI commands (preferred) and GUI alternative
      - What could go wrong and how to avoid it
      - How to verify it worked
      - Any performance or resource considerations

  - trigger: ":network-debug"
    replace: |
      Network problem: [DESCRIBE THE ISSUE]
      Topology: [VLAN setup / switches / firewall / etc]

      Give me a systematic debug approach:
      - Layer-by-layer diagnosis (L1 → L7)
      - Specific commands to run at each layer
      - Most likely culprits given the symptoms
      - Fix + how to document the resolution

  - trigger: ":explain-tech"
    replace: |
      Technical concept to explain: [CONCEPT]
      Audience: [MANAGER / NON-TECHNICAL COLLEAGUE / JUNIOR]

      Write a clear explanation with:
      - 1 analogy a non-tech person would understand
      - Why it matters in practical terms
      - What could go wrong if ignored
      - Max 150 words — be concise


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🎯 THINKING & DECISIONS
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":decide"
    replace: |
      I need to make a decision about: [DECISION]
      Options I'm considering: [OPTION A] vs [OPTION B]

      Apply the following frameworks:
      1. First principles thinking — break it down to fundamentals
      2. 10/10/10 rule — how will I feel in 10 minutes, 10 months, 10 years?
      3. Inversion — what's the worst that happens with each option?

      Give me a clear recommendation at the end.

  - trigger: ":brainstorm"
    replace: |
      Topic: [TOPIC]

      Give me 15 unconventional ideas. No obvious ones.
      For each: one sentence explanation + why it's non-obvious.
      Push the boundaries. Think like a contrarian.

  - trigger: ":challenge"
    replace: |
      I believe: [BELIEF OR ASSUMPTION]

      Act as a sharp critic. Challenge this belief aggressively.
      Give me: 3 strong counterarguments, 2 blind spots I might have,
      and 1 question that would make me reconsider everything.

  - trigger: ":reframe"
    replace: |
      Problem I'm facing: [PROBLEM]

      Reframe this problem in 5 completely different ways.
      For each reframe, suggest one unconventional approach to solve it.
      The goal is to make me see this from an angle I haven't considered.

  - trigger: ":stuck"
    replace: |
      I'm procrastinating on: [TASK]
      The real reason I think I'm avoiding it: [HONEST ANSWER]

      Give me:
      - The psychological reason I'm stuck (be direct)
      - A 2-minute starter action to break the block right now
      - How to structure the next 25 minutes to make real progress


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 💼 BUSINESS
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":bizidea"
    replace: |
      Evaluate this business idea: [IDEA]

      Give me:
      - Target market and real pain point it solves
      - 3 ways to validate it in under 2 weeks with $0
      - Main risks and how to mitigate them
      - Realistic revenue model
      - Similar existing businesses (so I can study them)

  - trigger: ":competitor"
    replace: |
      Company/product to analyze: [NAME]

      Break down:
      - Business model (how they actually make money)
      - Their biggest weaknesses and gaps
      - What users complain about most
      - 3 opportunities someone could exploit against them
      - Key metrics to track their growth

  - trigger: ":gtm"
    replace: |
      Product: [PRODUCT]
      Target audience: [AUDIENCE]
      Budget: [BUDGET]

      Create a go-to-market strategy for the first 90 days.
      Focus on: first 100 users, channels, messaging, and quick wins.
      Be specific — no generic advice.

  - trigger: ":email-cold"
    replace: |
      I'm reaching out to: [PERSON/COMPANY]
      My goal: [WHAT I WANT]
      My offer/value: [WHAT I BRING]

      Write a cold email. Max 5 lines. No fluff, no "I hope this finds you well".
      Subject line + body. Make it feel human, not templated.

  - trigger: ":trends"
    replace: |
      Industry/field: [FIELD]

      What are the 5 most underrated trends in this space right now?
      Not the mainstream ones everyone talks about — the ones that will matter
      in 2-3 years. For each: why it matters + one person/company already betting on it.


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🧠 LEARNING
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":learn"
    replace: |
      I want to learn: [SKILL/TOPIC]
      Current level: [ZERO / SOME BASICS / INTERMEDIATE]
      Time available: [X hours/week]

      Give me:
      - The 20% of knowledge that gives 80% of results (Pareto)
      - A 4-week learning roadmap
      - Best free resources (YouTube channels, docs, communities)
      - The #1 mistake beginners make in this field

  - trigger: ":summarize"
    replace: |
      [PASTE TEXT / ARTICLE / VIDEO TRANSCRIPT HERE]

      Summarize this in:
      - 3 bullet points (key takeaways)
      - 1 sentence (the core idea)
      - 1 action I can take today based on this

  - trigger: ":rabbit-hole"
    replace: |
      I'm curious about: [TOPIC]

      Take me down the rabbit hole. Give me:
      - The surface-level explanation (1 min read)
      - The deeper, non-obvious truth most people miss
      - 3 books, 2 YouTube channels, 1 subreddit to go deep
      - 1 mind-blowing fact that will make me want to explore more

  - trigger: ":inspire"
    replace: |
      I'm feeling stuck creatively. Topic/field: [TOPIC]

      Give me:
      - 3 websites or resources I've probably never heard of for inspiration
      - 2 creators (any medium) who think differently about this
      - 1 creative constraint or challenge to unstick me right now


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 🎬 MOVIES & SERIES
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":watchnext"
    replace: |
      I recently loved: [MOVIE/SERIES]
      What I liked most: [ACTING / PLOT / ATMOSPHERE / VISUALS / etc]
      Mood right now: [INTENSE / LIGHT / MINDBLOWING / EMOTIONAL / etc]

      Recommend 5 titles I probably haven't seen.
      For each: 2-sentence pitch and why it matches my taste.
      No mainstream obvious picks.

  - trigger: ":analyze-film"
    replace: |
      Film/Series: [TITLE]

      Give me a deep analysis:
      - Core themes and what the director was really saying
      - Symbolism and recurring motifs
      - Why it resonates (or doesn't) culturally
      - Most underrated scene and why it matters
      - What to watch next if I loved this


  # ─────────────────────────────────────────
  # 💪 LIFESTYLE
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────

  - trigger: ":workout"
    replace: |
      My goal: [LOSE FAT / BUILD MUSCLE / IMPROVE ENDURANCE / etc]
      Training days per week: [NUMBER]
      Equipment available: [GYM / HOME / CROSSFIT BOX / etc]
      Current level: [BEGINNER / INTERMEDIATE / ADVANCED]

      Build me a weekly training plan. Include sets, reps, rest times.
      Add a note on progressive overload strategy.

  - trigger: ":nutrition"
    replace: |
      My goal: [CUT / BULK / MAINTAIN / PERFORMANCE]
      Weight: [KG] — Activity level: [SEDENTARY / ACTIVE / VERY ACTIVE]
      Dietary restrictions: [NONE / LACTOSE FREE / etc]

      Give me: daily calorie target, macro split (protein/carbs/fat in grams),
      3 easy high-protein meal ideas, and 2 things to avoid.

  - trigger: ":recover"
    replace: |
      I'm experiencing: [MUSCLE SORENESS / FATIGUE / JOINT PAIN / POOR SLEEP / etc]
      Training frequency: [X days/week]

      Give me a science-backed recovery protocol for this week.
      Include: sleep optimization, nutrition timing, mobility work, and mental recovery.

  - trigger: ":trip"
    replace: |
      Destination: [CITY/COUNTRY]
      Duration: [X days]
      Travel style: [BACKPACKER / COMFORT / LUXURY / LOCAL EXPERIENCE]
      Interests: [FOOD / ARCHITECTURE / NIGHTLIFE / NATURE / etc]

      Build me a day-by-day itinerary. Include:
      - Hidden gems locals actually go to
      - What to skip (tourist traps)
      - Best neighborhoods to stay in
      - 3 food spots that are not on TripAdvisor top 10

  - trigger: ":weekend"
    replace: |
      I'm based in: Netherlands (Ede/Amsterdam area)
      Budget: [LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH]
      I want to feel: [ADVENTUROUS / RELAXED / INSPIRED / SOCIAL]

      Give me 5 weekend ideas I can do from here.
      Mix: local hidden spots + short train/flight trips in Europe.

  - trigger: ":plan-week"
    replace: |
      My main goal this week: [GOAL]
      Hours available outside work: [X hours]
      Energy levels tend to be: [HIGH MORNING / HIGH EVENING / CONSISTENT]

      Build me a realistic weekly plan. Block time for deep work, content creation,
      fitness, and recovery. Be honest about what's actually achievable.
